query: I need a snapshot of a remote environment I cannot log into directly before we touch anything.
/clairvoyance inspect that remote staging box and report what state it is in
Take a look at the inaccessible host and tell me what is running there right now.
Give me a non-invasive peek inside the sealed environment so I can see what is there without stepping into it.
Translate this vendor API jargon and weird protocol docs into plain operational meaning.
/comprehend-languages decode this DSL and tell me what it actually does
Help me understand this unfamiliar schema language and acronym soup without losing the technical meaning.
Make this foreign-looking spec readable to me, but do not redesign both sides to speak a shared protocol yet.
Before we act, surface any hidden automations, agents, or tool hooks attached to this workflow.
/detect-magic scan this stack for hidden AI or automation affordances
Show me what invisible helpers or integrations are already active around this system.
Reveal the hidden tool hooks and automation residue around this setup, not just files someone tucked out of sight.
Read this email thread and tell me the likely intent, priorities, and unstated concerns behind it.
/detect-thoughts infer what the stakeholder is really worried about from this message
What does this note seem to want, beyond the literal words on the page?
Parse the subtext of this message and tell me what they mean, not whether every claim is sourced and verifiable.
Load the right context and prep so the next debugging task starts strong instead of cold.
/enhance-ability front-load the key context before I start this review
Give me the minimum context pack and mental setup I need before I tackle this task.
Set me up with the right context for the work ahead, not a single precision shot at one exact command.
I need to inspect this system from inside the walls without changing state or leaving fingerprints.
/etherealness trace through the internals quietly and non-destructively
Walk through the hidden layers of this stack without interacting with them.
Let me pass through the internal structure of the environment for inspection, not just view it from a distant window.
Highlight the suspicious records and hidden hotspots so the team can focus attention fast.
/faerie-fire mark the risky lines and entities that deserve immediate review
Tag the important hidden things so they stand out in this messy data.
Make the hidden problem areas glow for review, rather than simply revealing concealed assets without prioritizing them.
Find the safest shortest route through this migration with the fewest traps and dead ends.
/find-the-path map the cleanest route through this setup maze
What sequence gets us from here to the goal with the least risk and wasted motion?
Show the route through this complex space, not just forecast what might happen after we pick one.
Estimate the likely outcomes and failure modes before we commit to this launch.
/foresight run the likely scenarios for this rollout before we approve it
Help me predict how this change is most likely to play out before we ship.
Model the probable outcomes of this decision, not the exact route to get there.
Explain what this mysterious service actually does and why it exists.
/identify inspect this unknown file and tell me its real function
I found a weird artifact in the repo; tell me what it is, how it behaves, and what it is for.
Tell me what this strange artifact does right now, not an exhaustive dossier on every mention of a named system.
Build me an exhaustive dossier on this named vendor and every relevant clue we can gather.
/legend-lore research this named project across all available sources
I need a deep background brief on this specific product, team, and its history before the meeting.
Research this named entity broadly and deeply, not just explain one unknown artifact in front of me.
Find what services are alive in this environment, where they live, and what resources they feed on.
/locate-animals-or-plants map the living components and what they consume
Show me the active ecosystem here: which living workloads exist and what each one depends on.
Map the living ecosystem and what it feeds on, not just one specific owner or running process.
Find the specific service owner or running process responsible for this behavior.
/locate-creature who owns this live service and where is it running
Track down the active process or person behind this issue in the larger system.
Find the live thing responsible here, not the static file or artifact that happens to mention it.
I know this config file exists somewhere; find it for me.
/locate-object find the exact dashboard query definition in this repo
Track down the specific artifact or file that contains this setting.
Locate the missing artifact itself, not the active service or person attached to it.
Reveal the things someone deliberately hid from ordinary browsing in this system.
/see-invisibility surface the obscured files flags and shadow paths
Help me find what is intentionally concealed or buried from normal view.
Expose what was deliberately hidden from sight, not every latent automation hook around it.
Extract useful knowledge from this deprecated system that nobody actively maintains anymore.
/speak-with-dead interrogate the legacy app for what it still knows
Pull answers out of the old abandoned service before we retire it.
Question the dead legacy system for what it remembers, not just reconstruct history from living records around it.
Read the slow growth signals in this ecosystem and tell me what the roots and branches imply.
/speak-with-plants interpret the long-horizon signals in this product ecosystem
Use gradual trend evidence to explain how this system has been growing and stressing over time.
Interpret the slow root-level signals in the ecosystem, not merely locate which living things are present today.
Make these two unlike systems understand each other without manual translation at every step.
/tongues bridge this API and that internal format so they can exchange meaning
I need a shared interface so both sides can speak the same operational sentence.
Create mutual understanding between the systems, not just translate one strange document for me.
Cut through the dashboards and spin and show me the ground truth of what is happening.
/true-seeing strip away abstraction and show the real state underneath
I want the actual reality here, not the polished story the tooling presents.
Pierce through the abstraction to ground truth, not merely label what one mysterious artifact is.
Gather the exact evidence so the next command lands correctly on the first shot.
/true-strike line up one precise move with evidence before execution
Help me aim this one high-stakes action so I do not miss.
Set up a single accurate strike based on evidence, not a broad context-loading session for general work.
Create a review mode where claims must be sourced and uncertainty is labeled explicitly.
/zone-of-truth run this meeting with sourced claims only
I need a high-integrity fact environment for this discussion before we decide anything.
Force sourced claims and explicit uncertainty in the room, not infer the hidden motives behind each speaker.
Help me make the first safe contact with this unfamiliar API before we ask it for anything ambitious.
/animal-friendship establish a gentle first handshake with this new service
I want the smallest trustworthy interaction with this vendor API so I can learn its temperament.
Start a cautious relationship with this unknown service, not bind it to a long-term contract yet.
Turn this spreadsheet into a self-updating artifact with triggers and a kill switch.
/animate-objects give this dashboard autonomous refresh behavior
Attach minimal automation so this passive artifact reacts when its inputs change.
Grant agency to the artifact itself, not create a separate background helper that does chores around it.
Wrap this old internal tool in a chat-style interface so people can ask it for what it already knows.
/awaken give this legacy database a conversational front door
Teach the crusty system to answer back without pretending it became smarter underneath.
Add an honest conversational wrapper to the old machine, not merely attach autonomous behavior to one passive file.
Help me jump cleanly from this branch to that environment without carrying unnecessary baggage.
/dimension-door switch me from one known workspace to the other with the right context
I need a fast reversible context switch between two known places.
Plan a tight context jump between known endpoints, not a full world-scale migration.
This pipeline is stuck on locks and dependency glue; diagnose the restraint and unblock it safely.
/freedom-of-movement cut the knot that is wedging this workflow
Find what is binding this process and show the least destructive way to get it moving again.
Unstick the workflow from its restraints, not focus only on recovering one missing key or permission token.
We are blocked by expired credentials and permissions; show the legitimate unlock path.
/knock rotate the key or request the access needed to open this door properly
Help me resolve this auth barrier through the right recovery or escalation channel.
Open the blocked access path through legitimate authorization, not just untangle general workflow friction.
This workflow already works; make it meaningfully faster without changing the outcome.
/longstrider speed up this build path without rewriting the product
Show me the highest-leverage optimization to shorten this path and keep correctness intact.
Increase pace on the same route, not brute-force a giant workload through with more endurance.
Make the smallest safe change to these records and show exactly what you touched.
/mage-hand apply one narrow edit with minimal blast radius
I need a delicate file or state manipulation, not a broad refactor.
Do a precise small manipulation on the object itself, not just craft an exact micro-patch pattern.
Design a standing worker that keeps performing one narrow job until I dismiss it.
/mordenkainens-sword spawn a persistent watcher with one mandate
I need a durable background process that repeatedly cuts through the same task.
Summon a narrow persistent worker with lifecycle controls, not a mundane helper handling assorted chores.
Bind this external API to a clear contract with scope, cost ceiling, and termination conditions.
/planar-binding define the service contract before we integrate this vendor
Constrain the outside service to a bounded role in our workflow before we rely on it.
Lock the external dependency into explicit terms, not just make a cautious first contact with it.
Convert this CSV schema into clean JSON while preserving the meaning.
/polymorph transform this interface from REST to GraphQL
I need a real format or protocol transformation from one representation into another.
Perform a reversible structural conversion, not an irreversible rewrite where the old form disappears.
Handle this quick utility cleanup without turning it into a whole project.
/prestidigitation tidy this small dataset and move on
Do the little practical task directly; it is not worth a larger workflow.
Treat this as a one-off utility trick, not a precise state manipulation on a specific object.
Plan the full cutover to move this service estate to the new environment with rollback.
/teleport migrate the whole application stack across with validation and fallback
I need a serious one-time migration plan that preserves continuity during the jump.
Move the whole world in one high-stakes cutover, not build a reusable route we will run every week.
Set up a reusable deployment pipeline between these environments with tests and monitoring.
/teleportation-circle build a standing sync route between source and destination
I need a permanent repeatable portal for code and data to travel through safely.
Codify a reusable route between environments, not just plan one dramatic migration event.
Design the irreversible rewrite where the old system form is gone and dependents must migrate.
/true-polymorph plan the permanent transformation with no rollback story
We are crossing the point of no return; help plan the permanent identity change.
Plan the one-way transformation that erases the old form, not a reversible format conversion.
Set up a quiet background helper to handle repetitive maintenance and log everything it does.
/unseen-servant run routine cleanup in the background and keep an audit trail
I want a silent persistent helper for mundane recurring tasks that I can dismiss anytime.
Create a reviewable chore-running helper, not a sharper single-purpose daemon that keeps cutting one target.
Add a few lightweight status signals so humans can see what this system is doing at a glance.
/dancing-lights hang tiny moving indicators on the important steps
I need small visible cues and heartbeat signals, not a full observability overhaul.
Add lightweight status beacons, not broad illumination of every dark corner with full logging and docs.
Adapt this explanation for executives without changing the underlying facts.
/disguise-self reframe this same message for a different audience
Change the presentation and tone for this audience while keeping the substance intact.
Tailor one presentation for one audience, not re-skin the entire cast across multiple surfaces.
Safely retire this active automation and all its hooks without leaving residue behind.
/dispel-magic decommission this monitor and integration cleanly
Help me shut down an active automation or watcher in a controlled way.
Cleanly decommission the active mechanism, not merely hush its alerts for a while.
Set an alert that fires when this boundary is crossed or this file changes.
/glyph-of-warding watch this resource and trigger when conditions are met
I need a monitor with a trigger condition guarding this boundary.
Place a watch that alerts on crossing conditions, not a named policy sigil that enforces the rule automatically.
Plan a live change that stays quiet to users while operators still see enough.
/greater-invisibility do this rollout with minimal audience-visible turbulence
I need to keep moving on a live system without drawing much attention from the audience.
Keep the live work going quietly in front of users, not just reduce incidental noise around ordinary operations.
Design layered defenses that make intruders work through multiple small barriers before anything valuable.
/guards-and-wards build a maze of small protections around this zone
I want defense in depth here, not a single tripwire.
Layer many defensive obstacles into a protective maze, not just place one boundary watcher on a single trigger.
Reduce the needless logs, chatter, and exposed surface around this legitimate workflow.
/invisibility quiet this operation without removing the audit trail
Lower the noise floor and incidental exposure here while keeping accountability intact.
Make the operation quieter overall, not keep a live user-facing change moving unnoticed.
This dark area of the system needs logs, explanations, and observability.
/light add visibility and explanation to this blind spot
Illuminate what is happening here with better logging or documentation.
Illuminate the dark zone broadly, not just hang a few tiny moving status cues.
Isolate this context so sensitive information cannot leak between tasks or sessions.
/mind-blank hard-separate these work contexts to prevent bleedover
I need stronger context isolation so one domain cannot contaminate another.
Prevent cross-session leakage through hard isolation, not just strip metadata from outputs.
Set up a convincing decoy endpoint so we can see who pokes at it.
/mislead place a false campfire and watch who approaches
I want a honeypot-style decoy that draws attention away from the real asset.
Create a false lure that observers interact with, not simply change how the real system is presented to them.
Strip unnecessary metadata and tracking from this flow to harden privacy.
/nondetection minimize exposure and remove tracking residue
Help me reduce discoverability by cutting metadata and passive data leakage.
Reduce metadata-based discoverability, not isolate whole contexts from each other.
Observe the live remote service in real time so we can see what it is doing.
/scrying watch that remote deployment live
I need live remote observation of the system while it runs.
Watch the remote service in real time, not merely translate its chirps and spikes after the fact.
Re-skin the whole product surface for this audience without rewriting the core roles underneath.
/seeming change the outward appearance of the whole cast consistently
I need a coordinated presentation layer shift across multiple surfaces, not a substantive rewrite.
Re-skin the whole cast coherently, not just tailor one message for one audience.
Suppress the irrelevant alerts and noisy channels so the team can focus.
/silence mute this alert storm and leave only the meaningful signals
Create a quieter working environment by filtering out the noise.
Cut the noise and chatter, not reduce the operational footprint of the underlying work itself.
Translate these chirps, spikes, and LED-style signals into plain language for me.
/speak-with-animals explain what these little status signals are saying
Interpret the noisy telemetry and simple status cues so a human can understand them.
Translate the simple signals into plain language, not just stare at the live remote system feed.
Mark this resource with an automatic policy trigger that fires when named conditions are met.
/symbol attach a policy sigil to this asset
I need rule-based enforcement tied to this specific resource and trigger condition.
Bind named policy enforcement to the resource itself, not just watch for a boundary crossing and alert me.
Which investigation spell should I reach for first before touching this messy situation?
notes: abstain/category-level: asks for category-level routing rather than a concrete spell

query: I need some kind of discovery or preflight help, but I do not know whether this is search, translation, or verification yet.
Which automation or action spell fits this job? I just know I need to change something safely.
notes: abstain/category-level: asks for category guidance, not a specific spell

query: I need help with access, automation, or migration, but I cannot tell which of those this really is yet.
notes: abstain/category-level: intentionally ambiguous within the category

query: I need some kind of monitoring or protection spell here, but I am not sure if this is observability, alerting, privacy, or stealth.
Which protective spell should I use to keep watch on this system without causing more noise?
notes: abstain/category-level: asks for general category guidance rather than a concrete spell
